about_us_title: Project Bruut: By and for the community
about_us_intro: Robotics in agriculture is often complex and unaffordable. We believe this can be different. Project Bruut was born from the desire to design a modular, open-source field robot that you, as a farmer or maker, can build, understand, and adapt yourself.
about_us_opensource_text: We want to make complex robotics accessible by providing a solid foundation. Because the project is open-source, you are not tied to expensive licenses or closed ecosystems. You have the freedom to fully customize the tool carrier to your own needs and share your innovations with others.
about_us_independence_text: No unnecessary electronics or overly complex systems. We use as many off-the-shelf standard components as possible. This means the robot is easy to build and, more importantly, that you can repair it yourself in the field without having to wait for an expensive technician.
about_us_reality_text: Let’s be honest: robotics is difficult. So don’t expect a ready-made commercial product wrapped up with a bow. What we do offer is a robust, programmable frame and a clear manual. We give you the tool—you give the robot its purpose.
about_us_community_text: Whether you’re a farmer with a welding machine or a developer who enjoys working with Ardupilot—we do this together. How great is it to share ideas as a community of enthusiasts, improve them, test them in the mud, and simply use them together?
about_us_conclusion: Help build the future of accessible agricultural robotics. Share your attachments, improve the code, or simply start building your own Bruut.
title: Welcome to Project Bruut
subtitle: Open-source robotics for the agricultural sector
about_text: Why open-source? Because we want to advance the agricultural sector with shared knowledge and modular technology.
footer_tagline: Simple agricultural robotics for everyone. Modular, electric, and fully open-source.
footer_copyright: © {year} Open Source Project Bruut. All rights reserved.
front_page_hero_description: An open source platform designed for farmers, makers, and innovators.
front_page_project_build_desc: Step-by-step guide on how to build the modular field robot using off-the-shelf components.
front_page_project_goal_desc: Discover our vision of making complex agricultural technology transparent and open-source.
front_page_project_functions_desc: Technical specs and possibilities for various attachments and autonomous control systems.
front_page_info_platform_title: Building the Future of Field Robotics
front_page_info_platform_desc: Project BRUUT is an initiative started by Jan Jaap Verweij, a Dutch dairy farmer and lead contributor to the open-source projects Cowcatcher and CalvingCatcher, where computer vision is transforming dairy farming; building on experience in software and electronics, the project expands into robotics to develop a simple, autonomous field robot that addresses key agricultural challenges such as reducing chemical use, lowering fuel and machinery costs, tackling labor shortages, and improving sustainability through applications like spot spraying, mechanical weed control, soil health improvement, and support for physically demanding work.
front_page_media_desc: A simple, affordable, and highly customizable solution. Whether you are a dairy farmer, arable farmer, or grow vegetables in greenhouses or in open fields, this tool carrier robot can be adapted to suit your needs and work seamlessly with 'community' or own machines.
front_page_feature_1_desc: No unnecessary electronics. Built with standard parts for easy maintenance, repairs, and expansion.
front_page_feature_2_desc: Adjustable width and length via a hole-bar system. Scalable for open fields, greenhouses, or gardens.
front_page_feature_3_desc: Scalable power from 400W to 5kW. Choose between 2-wheel or 4-wheel drive for maximum traction.
front_page_stats_subtitle: The modular power of open-source farming.
front_page_partner_1_desc: Proud partner and supporter of the open-source Bruut project.
front_page_partner_2_desc: Providing essential hardware and expertise for agricultural innovation.
front_page_partner_3_desc: Collaborating on sustainable and modular farming solutions.
func_hero_subtitle: The Bruut platform is a versatile tool carrier. Discover all modules and attachments you can add for specific tasks in the field, greenhouse, or yard.
func_1_desc: Mowing at various heights, including 'topping' plants above ground level.
func_2_desc: Targeted weed control with AI, reducing chemical usage and drift. Based on the 'Open Weed Locator' project.
func_3_desc: Front or mid-mounted blade for leveling terrain, pushing feed/grass in stables, or cleaning silo's.
func_4_desc: Precise application and spreading of granular fertilizers.
func_5_desc: Mechanical weed control using rotating fingers in light soil.
func_6_desc: Spreading lime to improve soil pH levels and structure.
func_7_desc: Measuring crop height and health to create task maps for nitrogen or phosphate.
func_8_desc: Front or rear-mounted seat, with the option to widen the frame tubes at the sides.
func_9_desc: Integrated panels to extend battery life and provide sustainable power during operation.
func_10_desc: Evenly spreading straw, specially designed for use between vegetable rows.
func_11_desc: Rear-mounted hitch for pulling trailers and other rolling equipment.
func_12_desc: Motorized wheelbarrow-style container for transporting and dumping loose materials.
func_13_desc: Large front-mounted brush or electrically driven radial brush for surface cleaning.
func_14_desc: Additional power supply modules to be installed between the frames.
func_15_desc: Auxiliary power source for additional electricity generation or mechanical power.
func_16_desc: Pumping water from ditches for local irrigation or crop spraying.
func_17_desc: Digging trenches to drain standing water from fields efficiently.
func_18_desc: Even distribution of sawdust or bedding material in stables.
func_19_desc: Machine for initial crop sowing or overseeding existing grassland.
func_20_desc: Transporting and processing compost within the main frame structure.
func_21_desc: Ballast weights to improve traction and vehicle balance for heavy tasks.
func_22_desc: Flexible arm with blades for mowing under fences and around obstacles.
func_23_desc: External hydraulic power source for specialized agricultural tools.
func_24_desc: Standard lifting system for attaching various agricultural implements.
func_25_desc: Soil cultivation specifically for vegetable gardens or light plots.
func_26_desc: Protective enclosure or housing for the operator.
func_27_desc: Improving soil structure and air supply in grassland.
func_28_desc: Measuring and mapping soil density at specific data points.
func_29_desc: Internal power unit within the frame for additional traction or electricity.
goal_hero_subtitle: Making complex robotics accessible by providing a solid foundation, powered by an active community of farmers and makers.
goal_feature1_desc: A platform where makers and enthusiasts share, improve, and test their designs, code, and attachments.
goal_feature2_desc: Thanks to electric drive and solar panel options, the robot is ideal for emission-free work in greenhouses and in the field.
goal_feature3_desc: Lighter and more efficient work for agricultural entrepreneurs, from large-scale farming to smaller vegetable gardens.
goal_info_title: From Base Frame to Your Own Application
goal_info_desc: We provide the tools and the setup. The farmer or maker then gives it a purpose, with independent control for endless possibilities.
robotbuild_hero_title: Build Your Own Bruut Field Robot
robotbuild_hero_subtitle: A step-by-step guide to assembling the modular chassis and drive system.
robotbuild_modular_desc: The robot features a highly modular framework using standard hole patterns. You can easily adjust the width, length, or height to perfectly fit specific field conditions and track widths. It's designed to be easily made and fabricated with full buildplans and pre made CNC-plans so any workshop can make it or cut and weld it yourself
robotbuild_hubmotors_desc: Powered by either 2 or 4 direct-drive hub motors (with 2 caster wheels for the 2WD setup). These maintenance-free motors provide incredible torque. Ranging from 400W to 3kW, you can scale the robot's size and capacity for heavier tasks while utilizing the exact same software.
robotbuild_maintenance_desc: Function over form: all crucial components are easily accessible through utility boxes on the robot. The system runs on a safe low-voltage battery setup (maximum 60V) with dedicated main switches. Computers and electronics are securely stored and protected inside these accessible compartments. Hardware can easily be isntalled yourself
robotbuild_customizable_desc: The open design is incredibly versatile. It can be easily expanded with a wide range of implements such as a seat, weed harrows, front blades, spray booms, or brooms, making it universally adaptable to any task.
robotbuild_control_sys_desc: Initially, the robot can be controlled via an RC remote or a built-in joystick—a vital fallback that will always remain available. The ultimate goal, however, is full autonomous operation, allowing the robot to work independently while you monitor it remotely.
robotbuild_ardupilot_desc: For RTK communication and autonomous driving, we currently leverage the robust open-source ArduPilot project. This provides reliable steering, precise mission planning, and efficient navigation across the field.
robotbuild_spot_spraying_desc: Our precision spot spraying capabilities are going to be built upon the Open Weed Locator (OWL) open-source software. Innovators as Guy Coleman is what open source community makes the best
